req.title: Manager views employee
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to view a employee through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-004 mandates that an authenticated manager can view an employee via the UI, that mandatory fields are validated, and that an audit record is written on success. Error paths cover 400 (validation), 401 (unauthenticated), and 403 (wrong role). The suite covers: (1) EP positive – valid manager views existing employee, audit record confirmed; (2) NEG – unauthenticated request returns 401; (3) NEG – authenticated non-manager role returns 403; (4) BVA – employee ID at boundary edge (minimum valid ID value); (5) DT – mandatory field absent triggers 400 validation error. State transition (authenticated → view employee → audit written) is implicitly traced in the positive case.

tests.name: test_manager_views_employee_success
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A known employee record exists with id=<EMPLOYEE_ID>
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_manager_token>
Navigate to GET /employees/<EMPLOYEE_ID> with the token
Capture HTTP status and response body
Query audit log for the view event
expected: Response body contains entity id matching <EMPLOYEE_ID>
Audit record with action='view_employee' and actor=manager is written
tests.executable:
def test_manager_views_employee_success(manager_token, existing_employee_id, audit_log_client):
    response = <api_get>(
        f"/employees/{existing_employee_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_token}"}
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert str(body["id"]) == str(existing_employee_id)
    audit = audit_log_client.latest_entry(
        action="view_employee",
        entity_id=existing_employee_id
    )
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must be written on successful view"
    assert audit["actor_role"] == "manager"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_employee
preconditions: A known employee record exists with id=<EMPLOYEE_ID>
actions: Send GET /employees/<EMPLOYEE_ID> without an Authorization header
expected: Response body contains an authentication error message
No audit record is written for this attempt
tests.executable:
def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_employee(existing_employee_id, audit_log_client):
    before_count = audit_log_client.count_entries(
        action="view_employee",
        entity_id=existing_employee_id
    )
    response = <api_get>(
        f"/employees/{existing_employee_id}"
    )
    assert response.status_code == 401
    body = response.json()
    assert "unauthorized" in body.get("error", "").lower() or \
           "authentication" in body.get("message", "").lower()
    after_count = audit_log_client.count_entries(
        action="view_employee",
        entity_id=existing_employee_id
    )
    assert after_count == before_count, "No audit record should be written"

tests.name: test_non_manager_role_forbidden_from_viewing_employee
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role=employee (not manager)
A known employee record exists with id=<EMPLOYEE_ID>
actions: Obtain a valid non-manager auth token via <get_employee_token>
Capture HTTP status and response body
expected: Response body contains a forbidden or insufficient-role error

tests.name: test_manager_views_employee_at_minimum_valid_id
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
An employee record exists at the minimum valid ID boundary (id=1)
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_manager_token>
Capture HTTP status and response body
Query audit log for the view event
expected: Audit record with action='view_employee' and entity_id=1 is written
trace: BVA: minimum valid employee ID boundary
tests.executable:
BOUNDARY_EMPLOYEE_ID = 1

def test_manager_views_employee_at_minimum_valid_id(manager_token, audit_log_client):
    response = <api_get>(
        f"/employees/{BOUNDARY_EMPLOYEE_ID}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_token}"}
    )
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert int(body["id"]) == BOUNDARY_EMPLOYEE_ID
    audit = audit_log_client.latest_entry(
        action="view_employee",
        entity_id=BOUNDARY_EMPLOYEE_ID
    )
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must be written at boundary ID"

tests.name: test_view_employee_with_nonexistent_id_returns_400_or_404
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
Employee ID 0 does not exist (below minimum boundary)
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_manager_token>
Capture HTTP status and response body
expected: Response body contains a validation or not-found error message
No audit record is written for entity_id=0
tests.executable:
def test_view_employee_with_nonexistent_id_returns_400_or_404(
    manager_token, audit_log_client
):
    invalid_id = 0
    before_count = audit_log_client.count_entries(
        action="view_employee",
        entity_id=invalid_id
    )
    response = <api_get>(
        f"/employees/{invalid_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {manager_token}"}
    )
    assert response.status_code in (400, 404)
    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("error") or body.get("message"), \
        "Error payload must include a descriptive message"
    after_count = audit_log_client.count_entries(
        action="view_employee",
        entity_id=invalid_id
    )
    assert after_count == before_count, "No audit record on failed view"
